title: Neomammillaria pectinata
taxonomy: Neomammillaria pectinata is classified as a species of cactus in the genus Mammillaria, which is a group of small to medium-sized cacti characterized by their globular stems and fine spines. The genus Mammillaria is further classified within the subfamily Cactoideae, which is a large and diverse group of cacti. Mammillaria species, including Neomammillaria pectinata, are native to the Americas, with a wide distribution across the southern United States, Mexico, and Central America. The classification of Neomammillaria pectinata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the cactus family. The exact classification of Neomammillaria pectinata may be subject to revision as new research and discoveries are made.
